flagged_rows.manual_notes: Over-abstention/retrieval miss: the corpus answers this directly, but IterKey retrieved hearing-chair/sanction chunks instead of the five-person panel chunk.
Incomplete: the answer covers acknowledging outside assistance but omits the benchmark requirement that the student’s academic position be truthfully reported at all times.
Over-abstention/retrieval miss: the corpus defines bribery directly, but IterKey retrieved an obstruction-process chunk instead.
Incorrect/retrieval miss: the answer says the retrieved evidence does not state the hearing notice period, but the benchmark chunk states at least ten business days.
Incorrect: the answer uses primary-hearing faculty authority, but the benchmark evidence assigns authority to determine Code violations to the Academic Integrity Hearing Board.
Over-abstention: retrieved chunks include the primary-hearing composition, so the cited evidence does not support the non-answer.
Incomplete/retrieval miss: the answer includes Ithaca-based locations and Cornell Tech, but omits Weill Cornell Medicine campuses from the benchmark evidence.
Unsupported answer: the corpus does not provide a blanket no answer to whether every PLOA can be extended for exactly two semesters; it only says colleges may set minimum and maximum durations.
Unsupported answer: the response conflates investigative interview recording and hearing-record audio language with the question of whether hearings are always video recorded.
